title: Pappobolus sagasteguii
intro: Pappobolus sagasteguii, also known as the Peruvian pappobolus, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pappobolus sagasteguii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
